You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: 6b1ba50b).

## Issue to Resolve

Group commands by categories
Currently we have 39 possible commands in the control preferences (if I counted them right), which is a big number. 

Besides searching, to find a command more easily, I suggest grouping them in some categories, like:

- Answer buttons
    - Show answer
    - Answer button 1
    - Answer button 2
    - ...
- Flags
    - red
    - blue
    - orange
    - ...
- Whiteboard
    - ...
- Current card/note
    - suspend
    - delete
    - bury
    - ...
- Navigation
    - Page up
    - Page down
    - Abort
    - ...
- Misc
    - whatever couldn't be put in any other categories

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Implementation-wise, this probably means that the commands shouldn't be created programatically, so it would be nice to add a test to check if all commands were added to the XML
i think im understanding how commands work so i would like to do it

- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`

 83→        get() = "binding_$name"

 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }

149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))

152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))

173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }

177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))

185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {

24→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
25→    override val preferenceResource: Int
26→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
27→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
28→        get() = "prefs.controls"
29→
30→    override fun initSubscreen() {
31→        val commandMappingCategory = requirePreference<PreferenceCategory>(R.string.controls_command_mapping_cat_key)
32→        addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(commandMappingCategory)
33→    }
34→
35→    /** Attaches all possible [ControlPreference] elements to a given [PreferenceCategory] */
36→    fun addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(category: PreferenceCategory) {
37→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
38→            val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
39→                setTitle(command.resourceId)
40→                key = command.preferenceKey
41→                setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
42→            }
43→            category.addPreference(preference)
44→        }
45→    }
46→}

40→        // Group commands by their CommandGroup
41→        val commandsByGroup = ViewerCommand.values().groupBy { it.group }
42→
43→        // Sort groups by their order and create categories
44→        val sortedGroups = CommandGroup.values().sortedBy { it.order }
45→
46→        for (group in sortedGroups) {
47→            val commands = commandsByGroup[group] ?: continue
48→

51→                setTitle(group.titleRes)
52→                key = "controls_category_${group.name}"
53→            }
54→            preferenceScreen.addPreference(category)
55→
56→            // Add all commands in this group to the category
57→            for (command in commands) {
58→                val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
59→                    setTitle(command.resourceId)
60→                    key = command.preferenceKey
61→                    setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
62→                }
63→                category.addPreference(preference)
64→            }

   - FLAGS - for flag-related commands
   - CARD_NOTE - for card/note management commands
   - WHITEBOARD - for whiteboard-related commands
   - OTHER - for miscellaneous commands

2. **Modified `ViewerCommand` enum** to include a `group` property that associates each command with a category.

3. **Updated `ControlsSettingsFragment`** to dynamically create `PreferenceCategory` for each `CommandGroup` and add commands to their respective categories.

4. **Updated `preferences_controls.xml`** to remove the single static category (which is now dynamically generated).

5. **Added string resources** for the new category titles in `10-preferences.xml`.

6. **Created a new test file** `CommandGroupTest.kt` to verify:
   - All commands are assigned to a valid group
   - All groups have at least one command
   - Command groups have unique order values

The solution groups the 37 commands into 5 categories:
- Answers (7 commands): Show answer, Answer buttons 1-4, Show Hint, Show All Hints
- Flags (8 commands): All flag-related commands
- Card / Note (11 commands): Edit, Mark, Bury/Suspend card/note, Delete, Tag, Card Info, Add Note, Reschedule
- Whiteboard (3 commands): Toggle, Clear, Change Pen Color
- Other (8 commands): Undo, Play Media, Exit, Page Up/Down, Abort and Sync, Record/Replay Voice

The implementation is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Added `CommandGroup` enum (`ViewerCommand.kt`)
Created a new enum to define the command categories:
- **ANSWERS** - For answer-related commands (Show Answer, Answer buttons 1-4, Show Hint, Show All Hints)
- **FLAGS** - For flag-related commands (all 8 flag commands)
- **CARD_NOTE** - For card/note management commands (Edit, Mark, Bury/Suspend card/note, Delete, Tag, Card Info, Add Note, Reschedule)
- **WHITEBOARD** - For whiteboard-related commands (Toggle, Clear, Change Pen Color)
- **OTHER** - For miscellaneous commands (Undo, Play Media, Exit, Page Up/Down, Abort and Sync, Record/Replay Voice)

### 2. Modified `ViewerCommand` enum (`ViewerCommand.kt`)
Added a `group` property to each command, associating it with the appropriate category.

### 3. Updated `ControlsSettingsFragment.kt`
Changed from adding all commands to a single category to dynamically creating a `PreferenceCategory` for each `CommandGroup` and adding commands to their respective categories. The categories appear in the order defined by the `order` property.

### 4. Updated `preferences_controls.xml`
Removed the static category element since categories are now dynamically generated.

### 5. Added string resources (`10-preferences.xml`)
Added category title strings:
- `pref_cat_answers` - "Answers"
- `pref_cat_flags` - "Flags"  
- `pref_cat_card_note` - "Card / Note"
- `pref_cat_other` - "Other"
- (Note: `pref_cat_whiteboard` already existed)

### 6. Created new test file (`CommandGroupTest.kt`)
Added tests to verify:
- All commands are assigned to a valid group
- All groups have at least one command
- Command groups have unique order values

This implementation addresses the issue by grouping the 37 viewer commands into 5 intuitive categories, making it much easier for users to find specific commands in the Controls preferences.

 36→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 37→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 38→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.gesture_answer_1),

 50→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 51→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 52→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),

 75→    companion object {
 76→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 77→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
 78→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 79→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    val preferenceKey: String
 83→        get() = "binding_$name"
 84→
 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
123→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
124→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
125→            when (this) {
126→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
127→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
128→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
129→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
130→                )
131→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
132→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
133→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
134→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
135→                )
136→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
137→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
151→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
153→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
154→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
157→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
158→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
159→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
161→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
162→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                else -> ArrayList()
171→            }

173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
187→    }

25→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
26→    override val preferenceResource: Int
27→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
28→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
29→        get() = "prefs.controls"
30→
31→    override fun initSubscreen() {
32→        addAllControlPreferencesToCategory()
33→    }
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Creates [PreferenceCategory] for each [CommandGroup] and attaches
37→     * all possible [ControlPreference] elements to their respective category
38→     */
39→    private fun addAllControlPreferencesToCategory() {
40→        // Group commands by their CommandGroup
41→        val commandsByGroup = ViewerCommand.values().groupBy { it.group }
42→
43→        // Sort groups by their order and create categories
44→        val sortedGroups = CommandGroup.values().sortedBy { it.order }
45→
46→        for (group in sortedGroups) {
47→            val commands = commandsByGroup[group] ?: continue
48→
49→            // Create a PreferenceCategory for this group
50→            val category = PreferenceCategory(requireContext()).apply {
51→                setTitle(group.titleRes)
52→                key = "controls_category_${group.name}"
53→            }
54→            preferenceScreen.addPreference(category)
55→
56→            // Add all commands in this group to the category
57→            for (command in commands) {
58→                val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
59→                    setTitle(command.resourceId)
60→                    key = command.preferenceKey
61→                    setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
62→                }
63→                category.addPreference(preference)
64→            }
65→        }
66→    }
67→}

 99→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
100→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
101→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
102→            collection.remove(element)
103→            collection.add(0, element)
104→            true
105→        }
106→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
107→    }
108→
109→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
110→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
111→            // do not reorder the elements
112→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
113→                return@BiFunction false
114→            }
115→            collection.add(element)
116→            return@BiFunction true
117→        }
118→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
122→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
123→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
124→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
125→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
126→    }
127→
128→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
129→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
130→        bindings.remove(binding)
131→        prefs.edit {
132→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
133→        }
134→    }

19→import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
20→import org.hamcrest.Matchers.greaterThan
21→import org.hamcrest.Matchers.hasSize
22→import org.junit.Test

24→/**
25→ * Tests for [CommandGroup] and the grouping of [ViewerCommand]s
26→ */
27→class CommandGroupTest {
28→
29→    @Test
30→    fun `all commands are assigned to a valid group`() {
31→        // Every ViewerCommand should have a valid group assigned
32→        // This verifies that commands are grouped by checking that every command's group exists in CommandGroup
33→        val validGroups = CommandGroup.values().toSet()
34→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
35→            assertThat(
36→                "Command ${command.name} should be assigned to a valid group",
37→                validGroups.contains(command.group),
38→                org.hamcrest.Matchers.`is`(true)
39→            )
40→        }
41→    }
42→
43→    @Test
44→    fun `all groups have at least one command`() {
45→        // Every CommandGroup should have at least one command
46→        val commandsByGroup = ViewerCommand.values().groupBy { it.group }
47→        for (group in CommandGroup.values()) {
48→            val commands = commandsByGroup[group] ?: emptyList()
49→            assertThat(
50→                "Group ${group.name} should have at least one command",
51→                commands.size,
52→                greaterThan(0)
53→            )
54→        }
55→    }
56→
57→    @Test
58→    fun `command groups have unique order values`() {
59→        val orders = CommandGroup.values().map { it.order }
60→        val uniqueOrders = orders.distinct()
61→        assertThat(
62→            "All command groups should have unique order values",
63→            uniqueOrders,
64→            hasSize(orders.size)
65→        )
66→    }
67→}
